title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, and Partitioning · Go Semantics Case: Ordering Throughput Tradeoff
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must scope order, partition by stable key, detect versions and gaps, and measure failover under bounded concurrency; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ordering throughput tradeoff increment in a keyed order and failover state machine in Go. Learners progres…
objectives: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Defend state with entity version, sequence, monotonic transition, deduplication, gap detection, and reconciliation rather than transport order alone; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Ordering Throughput Tradeoff · Theory Ordering Throughput Tradeoff
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4085 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure throughput, tail latency, skew, recovery, and correctness when selecting queue count, consumer concurrency, and order scope; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
